change: Wrapped batch completion in one Kysely txn (FOR UPDATE + guarded WHERE status='Active' flip), validated submitted members == actual membership rejecting dupes/omissions, and rejected completion while any productionEvent.endTime IS NULL; added pure assertBatchCompletionMembership guard + tests
task: 4/6: Serialize and harden batch completion path in edge function
assumptions: AC[4]: status is flipped to Completed as the last statement INSIDE the serializing transaction (before the post-commit issue/finish/GL edge-fn calls) rather than strictly after those effects — those effects run in separate edge functions that can only see committed rows and cannot join the Kysely txn, and the only non-Active status that blocks re-entry is the terminal 'Completed' (the jobOperationBatchStatus enum has no transitional value). The FOR UPDATE lock + guarded flip fully prevent the concurrent double-issue/double-GL that AC[4]/AC[5] target; strict effects-then-status ordering with retry-on-partial-failure would need a new intermediate enum state (schema change, out of this task's scope).

Aggregate batch timers and per-member slice events share the same (jobOperationBatchId, endTime IS NOT NULL) shape, so the delete+insert-slices must run inside the same transaction that first asserts no open timer exists — done.

change: batch-operations create/add claim operations atomically (WHERE jobOperationBatchId IS NULL + row-count assert) and updateWorkCenter now rejects terminal batches and any batch where production has started; added pure assertAllOperationsClaimed + assertBatchWorkCenterMutable guards with tests
task: 5/6: Make claim atomic and block work-center mutation after start
assumptions: AC[6]/grooming reference a status='Pending' that does not exist — the jobOperationBatchStatus enum is only Active/Completed/Cancelled and batches are created Active. Interpreted the intent as the PR reviewer's actual concern ('block work-center changes after production starts'): reject when status is not Active (Completed/Cancelled) AND when any productionEvent exists, mirroring the add/remove/dissolve guard.
Expected claim count uses the distinct set of requested ids (new Set(ids).size) so a duplicated id in the request payload does not spuriously fail the atomic-claim assertion.
